patterns_recognized: Home-favorite overconfidence persists: SEA@CHW (74%, May 8 loss) and NYM@ARI (68%, May 8 loss) confirm the model over-rates home favorites when market strongly disagrees; today's OAK@BAL (77.7%) and SEA@CHW (67.5%) repeat this setup.
Negative-edge PLATINUM/GOLD picks fail repeatedly: COL@PHI lost May 8 (edge -18pp, PHI GOLD); today COL@PHI repeats as PLATINUM with edge -15.4pp — same model, same direction, same warning.

Stage 1/Stage 2 >15pp gap signals bullpen-carry risk: OAK@BAL shows 36.2pp F5/full gap (OAK F5=41.5% vs full=77.7%), the most extreme on the slate; yesterday PIT@SF was downgraded for the same reason at 26.9pp.
Contrarian GOLD picks with F2_xwoba_gap have validated: CHC@TEX won May 8 (7-1) and was confirmed GOLD May 9 using the same signal; today the same setup fires a third consecutive day.
ATL@LAD edge collapses on calibration: model 65.6% drops to ~58% after calibration, which is below Vegas fair_prob of 62.4%; real edge is effectively zero or slightly negative.

WSH @ MIA.reasoning: fair_prob=39.0% is well below the 0.42 floor — Vegas prices MIA (Sandy Alcantara) at ~61%. WSH's +12.26pp model edge is outside the actionable band, and the large market lean against WSH is credible given Alcantara's quality. Stage 1/2 gap is 5.3pp (fine). Yesterday model picked MIA (flipped); today picks WSH but fair_prob still disqualifies. SKIP stands.

OAK @ BAL.reasoning: Stage 1/2 gap of 36.2pp (OAK F5=41.5% vs full=77.7%) is the largest on the slate — pick entirely depends on a late-innings bullpen thesis that cannot be verified. Model's 77.7% calibrates to ~61% per the [0.7,0.8] bucket, while Vegas has BAL at 55.2% (fair_prob=44.8% for OAK); real edge is modest. F1_xera_gap=1.29* is asterisk-flagged. Model probability jumped from 57% yesterday to 77.7% today — a 20pp swing in one day is suspicious. Matches SEA@CHW May 8 pattern (extreme edge, high confidence, bullpen-carry, lost).

COL @ PHI.reasoning: Heuristic #2 and #4 both trigger: PLATINUM tier with edge -15.4pp is an automatic downgrade. Vegas has PHI at 66.3% — model at 50.9% is deeply contrarian to a strong market favorite. Identical to COL@PHI on May 8: GOLD pick PHI, edge -18pp, COL exploded for 9 runs and won 9-7. Yesterday Claude also downgraded COL@PHI GOLD→SKIP (Kyle Freeland struggles away from Coors). Signals F1_xera_gap=3.99 and F3_swing_take_gap=261 appear already priced by Vegas. PLATINUM ~43% historical hit rate makes this doubly suspect.

LAA @ TOR.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: edge +3.14pp is below the 4pp floor and confidence_downgrade=True was pipeline-set. TOR at 67.4% raw calibrates to ~58%; fair_prob=64.3% (Vegas agrees on TOR direction). Stage 1/2 gap 12.2pp is within bounds. LAA@TOR confirmed SKIP on May 9 and May 10 by the model. No override rationale.

MIN @ CLE.reasoning: Grader assigned SKIP via tier logic; edge +13.69pp is within the [4,15]pp band but other grader rules capped the tier. Model 65.8% calibrates to ~58% per [0.6,0.7] bucket; fair_prob=52.1% implies ~6pp real edge. Stage 1/2 gap 10.2pp is clean. No negative postgame signals for this matchup. Pick direction looks reasonable but grader SKIP is appropriate without a GOLD elevation trigger.

SEA @ CHW.reasoning: SKIP already applied for extreme edge (+24.94pp). Critical context: SEA@CHW was the worst loss on May 8 — model had CHW at 74%, edge +31.2pp, SEA won 12-8. Yesterday CHW confirmed SKIP at 56.8%. Today's setup echoes May 8: model 67.5% vs fair_prob=42.5% (Vegas strongly backs SEA). The model may systematically over-rate CHW against SEA. SKIP stands; do not act against Vegas on this game.

NYY @ MIL.reasoning: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO is the strongest pipeline filter and is non-negotiable. Model's 50.3% and fair_prob=41.6% diverge (model slightly favors MIL, Vegas slightly favors NYY). May 8: NYY@MIL GOLD loss to Misiorowski (rookie SP heuristic). Yesterday Claude confirmed NYY GOLD based on Schlittler dominance (8 GS, 1.52 ERA). Today the model flips pick to MIL; with HARD_VETO active, the flip is irrelevant. SKIP stands unconditionally.

CHC @ TEX.reasoning: F2_xwoba_gap=0.023 is the same lineup-quality signal that fired correctly on CHC@TEX May 8 (won 7-1) and was confirmed again May 9 (GOLD confirmed, CHC direction with Jack Leiter struggling). Three-day consistent signal. Edge -4.52pp is above the -8pp trigger. Stage 1/2 gap is 17.5pp (F5=70.6%, full=53.1%) — CHC's late-game hold depends on bullpen; monitor bullpen health but the F5 dominance is strong enough to carry GOLD. Market has TEX at 57.7% (contrarian pick).

PIT @ SF.reasoning: Yesterday PIT@SF was downgraded to SKIP on a 26.9pp Stage 1/2 gap. Today the gap is only 3.4pp (PIT F5=65.9%, full=69.3%) — the bullpen-carry concern from yesterday is resolved. F2_xwoba_gap=0.032 (PIT lineup edge) is the primary signal. Model 69.3% calibrates to ~58% per [0.6,0.7] bucket; fair_prob=52.1% yields ~6pp real edge. SP info unverified — flag if PIT SP has fewer than 6 starts this season before acting.

STL @ SD.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: fair_prob=41.8% below the 0.42 floor. Stage 1/2 gap is 25pp (STL F5=79.3%, full=54.3%) — strong F5 edge evaporates in the full game, suggesting SD bullpen neutralizes STL late. May 8: STL@SD model was near 50/50, lost with SD. May 9: STL confirmed SKIP. Today model picks STL but fair_prob still disqualifies. Large Stage 1/2 gap adds further caution.

ATL @ LAD.reasoning: Calibration is the decisive factor: LAD 65.6% falls into the [0.6,0.7] bucket which calibrates to ~58% — below Vegas fair_prob of 62.4%. Real model edge is effectively zero or slightly negative. Edge +3.23pp is already below the 4pp floor, and confidence_downgrade=True was pipeline-set. F3_swing_take_gap=1646.6 is a strong signal but Vegas appears to already price it given LAD's 62.4% implied. May 8 LAD win was at +11.4pp edge — fundamentally different conviction.
